topic: LLM Session Compaction and Conversation Summarization
methodology: Direct source code analysis of local research repos and Cairn internals. No web fetching required — all sources are local codebases.

sources.title: Eino ADK Summarization Middleware
keyInsights: Trigger: total tokens > ContextTokens (default 190K). Checked in BeforeModelRewriteState middleware hook.
Token counting: pluggable TokenCounterFunc, defaults to 4-chars/token heuristic including tool schemas.
System messages are separated from context messages before summarization; system messages always preserved.
PostProcessSummary: replaces <all_user_messages> tag in summary with actual recent user messages (up to 1/3 of trigger budget).
TranscriptFilePath: optional path appended to summary so agent can re-read full context if needed.
Summary emitted as User role with two multi-content parts: summary text + continue instruction.
EmitInternalEvents: before/after summarize events emitted for observability.
Finalize hook: caller can fully customize final message list after summary is generated.
contentTypeSummary tag stored in message Extra field to identify prior summaries during user-message extraction.

sources.title: Eino Summarization Prompts
keyInsights: System instruction: 'You are a helpful AI assistant tasked with summarizing conversations.'

User instruction requests 9 structured sections: Primary Request, Key Technical Concepts, Files and Code Sections, Errors and fixes, Problem Solving, All user messages, Pending Tasks, Current Work, Optional Next Step.
All user messages wrapped in <all_user_messages>...</all_user_messages> XML block — this section is post-processed and replaced with real recent messages.
Summary preamble: 'This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context.'
Continue instruction: 'Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions.'
Transcript path instruction: 'If you need specific details from before compaction... read the full transcript at: %s'
Truncated marker format: '...N characters truncated...' (used for individual long user messages).
Bilingual (English + Chinese) via internal.SelectPrompt — language selected at runtime.

sources.title: Eino Tool Reduction Middleware
keyInsights: Two-phase: Truncation (immediate, after tool execution) + Clear (lazy, before LLM call).
Truncation: if tool output > MaxLengthForTrunc (default 50K chars), saves full content to backend, replaces with truncated preview + file path.
Clear: if total tokens > MaxTokensForClear (default 30K), iterates historical messages and offloads older tool call args+results to filesystem.
ClearRetentionSuffixLimit: number of most recent assistant-tool-call pairs to preserve from clearing (default 1).
Per-tool configuration via ToolConfig map — specific tools can have different truncation/clear handlers or skip either phase.
Offloaded content in /tmp/trunc/{call_id} (truncation) and /tmp/clear/{call_id} (clear).
Agent must have read_file tool pointing to same Backend — otherwise offloaded content is inaccessible.
msgReducedFlag extra field on messages marks which tool call groups have already been processed to avoid duplicate offloading.
Token caching: each message caches its token count in Extra[msgReducedTokens] to avoid recomputation.

sources.title: Gollem AutoContext — Auto-compress Messages
keyInsights: AutoContextConfig: MaxTokens threshold, KeepLastN recent messages (default 4), optional SummaryModel.
Token estimation: word-based (~1.3 tokens/word) via estimateStringTokens — more accurate than char/4 for mixed content.
currentContextTokenCount: uses max(estimatedTokens, lastProviderReportedInputTokens) — provider-reported is more accurate.
Summarization prompt: 9 explicit requirements including exact file paths, test pass/fail counts, constraint values, best result so far.
Individual messages truncated to 500 chars (user/assistant text) and 800 chars (tool results) in the summarization input.
Summary emitted as ModelResponse (assistant role) with '[Conversation Summary]' prefix.
BuildInstructionPin: key instructions from first message are extracted and prepended to summary to prevent drift.
stripOrphanedToolResults: drops ToolReturnParts without matching ToolCallPart, converts content to UserPromptPart '[Previous X result]' to preserve data without API violation.
RetryPromptParts (tool errors) also cleaned up via same mechanism.
Placeholder added if all parts removed to prevent consecutive assistant messages.

sources.title: Gollem Memory Strategies
keyInsights: Three strategies as HistoryProcessor functions (composable middleware pattern).
SlidingWindowMemory(windowSize int): keeps messages[0] + last windowSize*2 messages. Corrects start to maintain user/assistant alternation.
TokenBudgetMemory(maxTokens int): drops pairs from position 1 until under budget. ~4 chars/token estimation.
SummaryMemory(summarizer, maxMessages): splits at len-keepLast, summarizes middle, reconstructs. Uses model.Request not stream.
All three always preserve messages[0] (system prompt / first context).
SummaryMemory emits summary as ModelResponse (assistant role) so providers extracting system prompts to separate field still produce an API message.
Ensures recentStart points to a ModelRequest (user role) — summary is ModelResponse, so next must be user.

sources.title: Gollem Tool Output Truncation
keyInsights: TruncationConfig.MaxTokens: default 16000 (~64KB text).
Head/tail split: 60% head, 40% tail. Retains both beginning and end of output.
Line-boundary snapping: truncates at nearest newline to avoid mid-line cuts.
UTF-8 safety: backs up to last rune start byte at both cut points.

Marker: '

... [truncated N tokens] ...

' shows how much was dropped.
Returns original string if within budget — zero cost for normal-sized outputs.
bytesPerToken = 4 (same as Cairn's EstimateTokens).

sources.title: Plandex Conversation Summarization
keyInsights: Dual trigger: total tokens > GetPlannerEffectiveMaxTokens() OR conversation-only tokens > GetPlannerMaxConvoTokens().
Pre-computed summaries stored in DB as ConvoSummary with LatestConvoMessageId and LatestConvoMessageCreatedAt.
Token savings calculation: for each candidate summary, computes (conversationTokens - tokensUpToTimestamp[summary.timestamp] + summary.Tokens). Picks first that brings total under limit.
Fallback: timestamp lookup fails gracefully (logs, notifies, continues to next summary).
Summary in context: inserted as assistant message, then messages after summary timestamp appended after it.
Latest summary injected as assistant message mid-conversation even when no compaction occurs — reinforces current plan state.
Async: summarizeConvo called from tell_stream_finish.go after each reply completes.
Append-only prompt: 'Keep as much information as possible from the existing summary and add the new information from the latest messages.'

sources.title: Plandex Summary Prompt
keyInsights: Role: 'AI summarizer that summarizes the conversation so far... a plan to complete one or more programming tasks.'
Section 1: Summary of user messages, focused on tasks. Latest state of each task. Condense while retaining meaning.
Section 2: Key decisions, major changes, challenges, requirements/constraints.
Section 3: Latest messages and next steps.
Explicit constraint: 'Do not include code in the summary. Explain in words.'
Append-only instruction: critical for incremental summarization to avoid loss of early context.

sources.title: Google ADK-Go Session Model
keyInsights: ADK-Go does not implement in-process summarization — it delegates context management to the caller.
GetRequest.NumRecentEvents: limits events returned from storage to most recent N, implementing sliding window at storage layer.
SkipSummarization: flag on EventActions that prevents model-level function-response summarization.
IncludeContents: LLMAgent option — 'default' (full history) or 'none' (current turn only).
Branch: sub-agent isolation — agents in different branches don't see each other's history.
State scopes: app: (shared), user: (per-user), temp: (per-invocation, discarded), session-level (default).

sources.title: Google ADK-Go Database Session Service
keyInsights: NumRecentEvents implemented via ORDER BY timestamp DESC + LIMIT N, then reversed to ASC for response.
req.After: filters events to only those after a given timestamp — enables incremental session loading.
Stale session detection: compares request session's UpdatedAt with DB — prevents concurrent-write races.
State scoped transactions: app, user, session state all updated atomically with the event.
trimTempDeltaState: temp: prefixed state keys stripped before persisting to storage.

sources.title: Cairn Agent Types — Session.History()
keyInsights: Session.History() returns ALL events unconditionally — no compaction, no sliding window.
Events are []*Event; each Event has Parts []Part (TextPart, ToolPart, ReasoningPart).
eventToMessage converts: TextPart → TextBlock, ToolPart(completed, assistant) → ToolUseBlock, ReasoningPart → ReasoningBlock.
Tool results NOT included in History() — ToolPart with completed status on assistant events becomes ToolUseBlock only; tool results are separate role=tool messages.
The gap: no history size limit. A 100-round coding session will generate 100+ messages with potentially huge tool outputs.

sources.title: Cairn ReAct Agent Loop — Insertion Point
keyInsights: Line 72: messages := invCtx.Session.History() — correct insertion point for compaction (before user message appended at line 75).
Tool output appended at line 393-402 (messages = append(messages, llm.Message{Role: llm.RoleTool, ...})). Secondary insertion point for per-tool truncation.
Loop is per-round, not per-message — compaction should happen once per invocation at round 0, not every round.
Messages slice grows: starts with history, then each round adds assistant message + N tool results. After 20 rounds with 3 tools each, that's 60+ new messages on top of history.
Max rounds: talk=10, work=10, coding=100. Coding mode is the critical path for context overflow.

sources.title: Cairn ContextBuilder — Memory Token Budget
keyInsights: memory.Compact() is for semantic memories (memory_items table), not conversation messages. Uses confidence decay, not summarization.
ContextBuilder manages system prompt injection budget (default 4000 tokens): hard rules → RAG memories → journal → soul.
EstimateTokens: (len(text)+3)/4 — same formula as all frameworks.
Stage 3 journal digest: last 48h of session journal entries (summaries of past sessions), outside memory budget.
Compaction here = discarding old unused facts from the memory store. This is orthogonal to session history compaction.
The memory system is complete and correct. The gap is purely in the conversation history path.

sources.title: Cairn Memory Service — Compact()
keyInsights: Compact() method: decays memories with UseCount=0 and age > 30 days by factor 0.8. Auto-rejects if confidence < 0.1.
This is memory store maintenance, NOT conversation compaction.
BackfillEmbeddings: batch re-embeds accepted memories without vectors, with rate limiting.
Memory lifecycle: proposed → accepted (user/agent approved) → potentially rejected (Compact or user action).

gaps: No web sources — all analysis is from local codebases. Academic literature on conversation summarization (e.g., DialogLM, SUMBT) not covered.
Anthropic's native context caching (prompt caching) not covered — could be relevant as a complementary strategy.
No benchmarks on summarization quality degradation over multiple compaction cycles.
